iOS & Android
Built for the bench
The problem

ELNs capture what happened.
Nobody captures why.

Every ELN records the result — the number, the band, the measurement. But the result alone never tells the full story. The conditions that produced it, the deviations that explain it, the observations that contextualize it — those disappear the moment the experiment ends.

What ELNs record today

Quantitative data

Numbers, results, measurements. The output. Structured, searchable, well-served by every ELN on the market.

📊
Assay results & measurements
IC50, OD readings, band intensities, cell counts — the numbers are captured.
🧬
Protocol steps (as written)
The procedure on paper. What you were supposed to do.
📅
Date, scientist, experiment ID
Who ran it, when. The metadata shell.
What Probe captures

Qualitative data

Conditions, context, deviations, observations. The story behind the result. What actually happened at the bench.

🧪
Reagent lots & passage numbers
"Same antibody lot as last time — cells at passage 36 today." Captured as you say it.
⚠️
Real-time deviations
"Incubation ran 20 minutes long, had to take a call." The deviation that explains the outlier.
💡
Observations & intuitions
"Cells looked slightly stressed before plating — might explain the low viability." Never written down. Until now.
🌡️
Environmental conditions
"Lab was unusually warm today, incubator fan sounded off." The invisible variable.
How probe works

Three steps.
None of them typing.

Probe works the way scientists already work — by talking. You already narrate your experiments to yourself at the bench. Probe just finally listens, structures what you say, and makes it searchable forever.

1
Open Probe. Hit record. Start your experiment.

That's it. Talk the way you already talk at the bench — informal, incomplete, mid-thought. "Starting the western, antibody is the same lot as last time, cells are passage 36..." Probe understands scientific language without you formatting anything.

Works in gloves Background recording No wake word
2
Probe extracts entities and structures everything you said.

Your spoken words become a structured qualitative record. Reagent lots are tagged. Passage numbers are linked. Deviations are flagged. Observations are preserved. You review a clean 10-second summary — not a form.

Lot extraction Deviation tagging Passage tracking Entity linking
3
Your lab builds a qualitative memory it can query forever.

Every experiment adds to a living, searchable record of your lab's qualitative knowledge. Ask in plain language months later. "What conditions were we running last time BRCA1 editing underperformed?" Probe knows. Even after the person who ran it has left.

Plain language search Permanent record Survives turnover
What you're building

A qualitative record
no lab has ever had.

After 6 months with Probe, your lab has something genuinely new: a searchable archive of the context behind every experiment — the conditions, the deviations, the observations that never made it into any ELN.

Phase 1 — live now

Probe Phase 1 captures qualitative data only — the spoken, contextual layer of science. We're being explicit about this because it matters. Quantitative data integration — instrument outputs, numerical results — comes in Phase 2. Early adopters help shape what that looks like.

Questions your lab can answer with Probe
Lab memory
"Why did our BRCA1 editing efficiency drop in September?"
Probe surfaces: lot #LT-2290 introduced Sept 3, used in 8 of 11 experiments that month. 6 noted cold-temperature deviations. Same lot flagged in 3 researcher notes as "looks cloudy."
Researcher memory
"What did Dr. Chen observe about passage sensitivity before she left?"
Probe finds 14 voice logs from Dr. Chen across 8 months referencing passage effects — all structured, tagged, and still queryable two years after her departure.
Protocol memory
"What's actually different between how we ran this in Q1 vs Q3?"
Probe compares qualitative records across time periods: 3 protocol deviations consistently present in Q3 that weren't logged anywhere — only mentioned in voice recordings.
Who it's built for

Built for the people who
actually run the science.

Grad student & postdoc
The one at the bench

You're running 90% of the experiments and logging 10% of the context. Not because you're lazy — because stopping to type while gloved, mid-protocol, is impossible. Probe logs as you work.

"I talk through my experiments anyway. Now it actually listens."
Principal Investigator
The one who needs it to last

A paper revision arrives 18 months later. The postdoc who ran the experiment is gone. Their notebook is cryptic. Their scripts don't run. Probe is the institutional memory that doesn't leave when they do.

"It's like having a lab manager who never leaves and never forgets."
Head of biology · Early biotech
The one making the calls

Go/no-go decisions on programs made with PowerPoint summaries of data nobody can trace back to raw conditions. Probe gives you the qualitative layer — what actually happened in the experiments your decisions rest on.

"Finally a record of what actually happened — not just what we meant to do."
Where we're going

Phase 1 is the
beginning.

We're being transparent about what Probe does today and where it's headed. Early adopters shape the roadmap — and get in before it becomes the standard.

1
Live now
Qualitative data capture

Voice-first experiment logging. Probe listens at the bench, captures qualitative context, structures it automatically, and builds your lab's searchable memory.

  • Voice recording & transcription
  • Qualitative entity extraction
  • Lot, passage & deviation tracking
  • Plain language search across all experiments
  • iOS & Android mobile app
2
Coming next
Quantitative data integration

Real numerical data — instrument outputs, assay results, measurements — integrated alongside qualitative records. The full picture of every experiment.

  • Instrument data ingestion
  • Numerical result linking
  • Qualitative + quantitative correlation
  • Cross-experiment pattern detection
3
Long term
The brain — pre, during & post

Probe learns your lab deeply enough to advise before you run an experiment, flag anomalies during it, and explain outcomes after — grounded in your own data.

  • Pre-experiment risk scoring
  • Real-time guidance during runs
  • Post-experiment root cause analysis
  • Predictive protocol optimization

Be the first lab
to never forget.

Early access is open for select academic and biotech research labs. iOS and Android. Qualitative data capture, live now.

Currently onboarding labs at —
Harvard · Stanford · CMU · Columbia · Johns Hopkins · Georgetown
No commitment. We'll reach out when you're next in the queue.
